problem_statement: The industry response to AI-generated code quality problems is to deploy AI reviewers, but without external executable specifications the generating and reviewing agents share the same training distribution and blind spots, so review checks code against itself rather than against intent.
magnitude_evidence: DORA 2026 report, based on 1,110 open-ended survey responses from Google engineers, found higher AI adoption correlates with higher throughput and higher instability simultaneously; time saved generating code is re-spent auditing it.
insufficiency_evidence: AI review ranged from 0% to 100% detection depending on domain opacity; the ICD-10-CM external cause code rule was missed by all four models at 0/5; on the ICAO fuel reserve case, in all five runs Claude confidently asserted the swapped values as correct ICAO rules and declared the implementation correct.
solution_mechanisms: executable specifications as deterministic quality gate
specification-first then deterministic verification pipeline then AI review
cross-family reviewer diversity plus external grounding
architectural tooling (ArchUnit, Dependency Cruiser) and contract testing (Pact) for structural rules
runtime verification / observability for timing/concurrency defects
AI review scoped to unarticulated architectural residual
user testing/design thinking for specification defects
key_constructs: Cynefin domain transition (enabling to governing constraints)
